danger_signals.visual_form: Cat character in themed costumes matching stage environment

danger_signals.meaning: Secondary threats requiring avoidance or distraction

danger_signals.visual_form: Warning chime sound + music speed increase
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent stage failure; player taking too long to collect items

danger_signals.visual_form: Additional coin-shaped threat chasing Mappy after time expires
danger_signals.meaning: Critical danger state; cannot be distracted or attacked, stage failure imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Trampolines break after repeated jumps (except Jungle World)
danger_signals.meaning: Resource depletion danger; loss of mobility/escape options

safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety from Meowkies; enemy will dance harmlessly around item
Temporary safety from Goro specifically; primary boss becomes harmless

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible fish bouncing in stage direction
safety_signals.meaning: Redirect both Goro and Meowkies away from player

safety_signals.visual_form: Specific trampoline in stage that launches upward when jumped without input
safety_signals.meaning: Safe access to bonus area for additional lives and items

safety_signals.visual_form: Rightmost stage exit after item collection
safety_signals.meaning: Safe stage completion when all six target items collected

safety_signals.visual_form: Player-controlled flashlight beam in Ghost Town stage
safety_signals.meaning: Ward off ghost enemies; stage-specific safety mechanism
Alternative mobility and hazard avoidance in ghost-themed stage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mouse character; originally police-themed in arcade version
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Goro and Meowkies are opposing cat faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Target item collection missions (presents, wedding rings, Christmas trees, baseballs) indicate law/order objectives

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Costume changes per environment (Railroad, Western, Tropical, Jungle, Pirate, Ghost, Urban, Space themes)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Goro's costume adaptation to each stage signals faction presence and thematic ownership

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cat gang members; visual variants not specified
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Responsive to different distraction items (toys, vines) suggesting hierarchy under Goro
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Gang presence throughout all stages; expendable compared to Goro

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mappy navigates to collect cheese; dialogue/narrative frames gift-giving context

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wedding rings replace cheese as collectible items; romantic/ceremonial stage theming
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mappy collects rings; relationship milestone progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Christmas trees become target items; holiday visual theming in environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Seasonal celebration context; same mechanical gameplay with narrative layer

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Baseballs replace other items; child-oriented stage design; family unit established
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Paternal responsibility narrative; bonus Milky Town castle finale with family reunion mechanic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Train/rail-themed stage architecture; Goro wears railroad conductor outfit
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage-specific enemy costume signals thematic ownership; terrain reflects industrial setting

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Desert/saloon theming; Goro wears western cowboy costume
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Thematic enemy adaptation; stage hazards/layout match western environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Palm trees, beach theming; Goro tropical costume variant
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Exotic environment signals faction territorial expansion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dense vegetation, vine theming; Goro jungle explorer costume; trampolines do not break (exception rule)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unique stage mechanic signals world-state difference; infinite mobility resource

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ship/nautical theming; Goro wears pirate costume
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aquatic hazard environment; themed enemy costume

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Haunted/spectral theming; ghost enemies replace standard threats; Goro ghost costume; flashlight mechanic present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Supernatural danger state; flashlight as stage-specific tool; balloon transport vehicle available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urban/city environment theming; Goro urban costume variant
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Civilized zone; different threat behavior from wilderness stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Space/milk-themed aesthetics; castle finale location established
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bonus castle finale unlocks; must deliver collected items to Mapico/Mappy Jr. before music ends; family reunion mechanic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual music acceleration cue; warning chime audio signal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Goro Coin spawns as unstoppable pursuer; stage becomes unwinnable if items not collected

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Specific trampolines glow/indicate launch capability when approached with zero distraction items
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player launched upward to hidden bonus stage; extra lives and items awarded

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Same stage layouts return with visual complexity increase implied
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy speed increases; difficulty escalation without new visual assets

environmental_storytelling.example: Goro wears railroad conductor in Railroad Town, cowboy in Western World, pirate in Pirate World, etc. Signals territorial control and thematic integration
Cheese (birthday), wedding rings (marriage), Christmas trees (holiday), baseballs (child). Each story arc uses collectibles to communicate narrative progression
Eight distinct visual environments (railroad, western, tropical, jungle, pirate, ghost, urban, space) show world expansion and faction territorial reach
Cat toys affect Meowkies, gold coins affect Goro, silver vines incapacitate, fish redirect both. Item visual type communicates mechanical effect
Milky Town castle represents family home/destination; final stage narrative payoff location where Mapico/Mappy Jr. await item delivery
Flashlight and balloon tools appear only in Ghost Town; visual/mechanical differentiation signals supernatural world-state
Warning chime + music speed increase + Goro Coin spawn create cumulative danger signal chain indicating imminent failure state
Repeated jumps cause trampolines to break (except Jungle World); visual depletion of mobility assets increases danger

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Pursues Mappy; affected only by Gold Coins and Fish distractions; immune to Cat Toys and Silver Vines
Stops pursuit and dances around gold coin; harmless to Mappy during effect duration
Chases fish instead of player; redirected threat
Transforms into Goro Coin; becomes unstoppable pursuer that cannot be distracted or attacked
Pursue Mappy in group; affected by Cat Toys, Silver Vines, and Fish; not affected by Gold Coins
Dance around toys harmlessly; unable to pursue
Lose consciousness temporarily; incapacitated threat state
Chase bouncing fish; redirected from player pursuit
Replace standard cat enemies in Ghost Town stage; warded off by flashlight mechanic
Repelled by flashlight beam; harmless when illuminated
Can jump to collect target items and activate/trigger stage-specific counterattack items
Can hold up to 15 distraction items; capacity limiting mechanic affects strategy
Must have zero distraction items and jump on specific trampoline without input; success launches upward to bonus stage
Must deliver all collected items to Mapico or Mappy Jr. before music ends; failure triggers retry loop; success grants bonus and story progression

notes: Mappy-Land uses stage-themed costume changes for Goro as primary world-state indicator across eight distinct environments. Distraction item types directly communicate enemy-specific vulnerability, creating a systematic threat communication language. The narrative structure (Story 1-4) uses collectible item changes rather than visual world transformation—meaning the world-state is communicated through objectives and dialogue rather than environmental visual change. Family progression narrative (birthday → marriage → holiday → child) frames player advancement. Ghost Town stage is unique departure with supernatural theming, flashlight mechanics, and balloon transport, signaling major world-state transition. Time pressure system uses audio (warning chime, music acceleration) before visual escalation (Goro Coin spawn). Japanese-to-US regional differences noted: title screen color changes (yellow→green), UI additions (collection counter), and ending text rewrite suggest localization impact on narrative communication but minimal impact on core manifestation mechanics.
